label: Monte Carlo tree search

node.label: Monte Carlo tree search
node.definition: Monte Carlo tree search alternates selection, expansion, simulation, and backup to allocate search effort toward promising actions under uncertainty.
node.why_it_matters: The role of Monte Carlo tree search is to connect Graph search, BFS/DFS & A*, Probability theory to downstream capabilities including World-model rollout & imagination, Game-playing, control & simulation agents.
examples: substrate for Game-playing, control & simulation agents
limitations: Search and self-evaluation can multiply cost while preserving confident errors when states, tools, or verifiers are weak. Its direct dependencies include Graph search, BFS/DFS & A*, Probability theory.

dependencies.rationale: Monte Carlo tree search explores alternatives using the state, scoring, or search process in Graph search, BFS/DFS & A*.
Monte Carlo tree search explores alternatives using the state, scoring, or search process in Probability theory.
Monte Carlo tree search explores alternatives using the state, scoring, or search process in Game theory & decision theory.
